description: Running record of engineering lessons from building agentctx. Ordered newest-first within each phase.

entries.lesson: Sanitise reasoning traces, not just outputs
entries.context: CoT steps surface PII into intermediate reasoning text that is stored before any output filter runs; agentctx persists these traces in observational memory and checkpoints.
entries.resolution: Apply the same (or stricter) PII sanitisation pipeline to reasoning-trace segments before they are written to any agentctx store, treating them as untrusted input even when they originate from a trusted model.
entries.rule: Never write an unsanitised reasoning trace to a persistent or shared store; treat CoT output with the same suspicion as raw user input.

entries.lesson: Fleet memory is a PII amplification path
entries.context: A single agent's CoT leakage can propagate to all fleet-memory subscribers if cross-agent boundaries do not enforce redaction at commit time.
entries.resolution: Add a redaction gate at fleet-memory write boundaries: classify each context entry's PII risk level before broadcast and strip or mask tokens above the configured risk threshold.
entries.rule: Cross-agent context sharing must be gated by PII risk classification; high-risk tokens must never reach the shared bus unredacted.

entries.lesson: Use hybrid, pluggable gatekeeper chains rather than a single detector
entries.context: The paper shows no detector dominates across model families and reasoning budgets; a fixed single method will have systematic blind spots.
entries.resolution: Design agentctx's sanitisation API as an ordered list of callable gatekeepers so operators can compose rule-based, NER-based, and LLM-judge steps and tune the ensemble for their specific model and risk profile.
entries.rule: Never hardcode a single PII detection method; expose a composable, ordered pipeline interface.

entries.lesson: Treat reasoning budget as a privacy-relevant, auditable parameter
entries.context: Leakage severity is a function of CoT budget and model family; an unconstrained reasoning budget is an uncontrolled privacy variable.
entries.resolution: Expose max_reasoning_tokens as a first-class, per-agent agentctx configuration parameter, log it alongside each run's audit trail, and surface it in context-engineering recommendations.
entries.rule: Reasoning budget must be declared, bounded, and audited — not left as an implicit model default.

entries.lesson: Single-point hash verification leaves earlier audit entries vulnerable to undetected tampering
entries.context: AuditLog.verify() compares current content against only the last recorded SHA-256 digest; a malicious actor could alter prior entries without triggering any alarm.
entries.resolution: Adopt a hash-chain scheme where each AuditEntry records the SHA-256 of the previous entry's digest alongside its own, making any retroactive modification detectable.
entries.rule: Tamper-evidence requires a full hash chain, not just a terminal hash; never rely on a single checkpoint to assert log integrity.

entries.lesson: Capability-scoping terminology in agentctx does not align with widely-used industry patterns, reducing documentation discoverability
entries.context: The TrustTier / spotlight / Sanitizer system is functionally equivalent to what the industry calls an 'AI Firewall', but the codebase uses internal terminology only.
entries.resolution: Add cross-references in docstrings and the PRD mapping agentctx concepts (TrustTier, sanitize_for_observation, spotlight) to the canonical pattern names (AI Firewall, Prompt Injection Defence).
entries.rule: Use industry-standard pattern names as aliases alongside internal naming so that external developers can map their mental model to the library surface.

entries.lesson: Bounded Autonomy requires an explicit runtime gate, not just an intent anchor
entries.context: The Anchor class records original task intent but provides no mechanism to halt execution or request human confirmation when a proposed action crosses a risk threshold.
entries.resolution: Introduce a requires_human_approval(action_metadata) hook in RunState that downstream orchestrators can wire to a human-in-the-loop approval flow before high-impact tool calls proceed.
entries.rule: Storing intent is necessary but not sufficient for Bounded Autonomy; the framework must also expose a decision-gate primitive that orchestrators can intercept.

entries.lesson: Fleet-memory trust boundaries do not substitute for time-bounded credential scoping (JIT privileges)
entries.context: agentctx's cross-agent trust tiers control what one agent can read or write in shared memory, but they are permanent for the session duration — there is no TTL or auto-revocation.
entries.resolution: Extend fleet-memory trust-tier assignments with an optional expires_at field so that elevated inter-agent access automatically degrades to a lower tier after task completion.
entries.rule: Trust tiers and JIT privileges are orthogonal controls; scope restriction alone is insufficient without temporal expiry.

entries.lesson: Linear context accumulation causes Working Memory Bottleneck in long-horizon tasks
entries.context: End-to-end MLLMs evaluated on hour-to-month scale video degrade when their context windows saturate, because all observations are appended rather than summarised.
entries.resolution: ReMA iteratively updates a compact recursive belief state, discarding raw observations after integration.
entries.rule: agentctx observational memory MUST compress older observations into a rolling summary; never allow unbounded linear growth of the raw observation buffer.

entries.lesson: Agents lose positional coherence on sparse long timelines without explicit anchoring
entries.context: Agentic baselines collapsed globally when navigating month-long sparse timelines because no positional state was maintained across steps.
entries.resolution: Checkpoints must include explicit temporal/positional anchors so agents can re-orient after any interruption or context reset.
entries.rule: agentctx run-state checkpoints MUST serialise a temporal anchor (e.g., step index, wall-clock timestamp, logical position) alongside task state; restoration logic must reload this anchor before resuming.

entries.lesson: Memory architecture must be calibrated to context density, not just context length
entries.context: Day/Week/Month splits in MM-Lifelong expose qualitatively different failure modes depending on how densely events are packed in time, not just the total duration.
entries.resolution: Configurable retention policies (high-fidelity recent window + compressed long-term summary) rather than a single eviction strategy.
entries.rule: agentctx SHOULD expose a density-aware retention policy parameter so library users can tune memory fidelity to their agent's operational tempo.

entries.lesson: Visual concealment of injected text is only one attack surface; plaintext injections account for 37.8% of real-world cases.
entries.context: Sanitisation logic that focuses exclusively on hidden-text heuristics (zero-font, off-screen) will miss the majority of injection attempts that embed malicious instructions in visible but contextually plausible prose.
entries.resolution: Supplement structural concealment detection with semantic anomaly detection — flag content that contains instruction-like imperative syntax ('ignore', 'instead', 'you are now') when sourced from untrusted zones.
entries.rule: Never treat sanitisation as purely a formatting problem; instruction-pattern matching on visible text is equally important.

entries.lesson: Base64 and XML/SVG encapsulation allow payloads to bypass naive string-matching sanitisation.
entries.context: Attackers encode malicious prompts as Base64 strings that are decoded and injected into the DOM at runtime, making static pre-fetch scanning ineffective.
entries.resolution: agentctx's sanitisation pipeline should recursively decode all known encoding schemes (Base64, URL-encoding, HTML entities) and re-run the sanitisation pass on the decoded output before context assembly.
entries.rule: Sanitisation must be applied post-decoding, not pre-decoding.

entries.lesson: Spotlighting — structurally tagging trusted vs. untrusted content in the context window — is the highest-leverage architectural mitigation.
entries.context: Without structural separation, the LLM cannot reliably distinguish system-level instructions from adversarially injected instructions embedded in retrieved web content.
entries.resolution: Enforce a mandatory trust-tier tagging scheme in agentctx's context engineering layer, wrapping all externally sourced content in clearly demarcated XML tags (e.g., <untrusted_web_content>) that are also referenced in the system prompt's meta-instructions.
entries.rule: Context assembly must always encode provenance; the model must never receive a flat, undifferentiated context window.

entries.lesson: Run-state checkpointing provides a post-hoc mechanism to detect injection-driven goal hijacking.
entries.context: An agent that has been successfully injected will begin executing actions (donations, file deletions, phishing promotion) that deviate from the user's original intent, but no intermediate signal may surface this deviation.
entries.resolution: Encode the user's stated goal as an intent fingerprint at session start and compare each planned action against it at checkpoint boundaries; flag or block actions with low semantic similarity to the original goal.
entries.rule: Checkpoints should validate intent alignment, not just execution state.

entries.lesson: Test doubles belong in src/, not tests/
entries.context: FakeLLMAdapter was initially written in tests/conftest.py. Any test file that tried `from tests.conftest import FakeLLMAdapter` got ModuleNotFoundError because tests/ has no __init__.py and pytest does not add it to sys.path as a package.
entries.resolution: Moved FakeLLMAdapter to src/agentctx/testing.py. Now importable as `from agentctx.testing import FakeLLMAdapter` everywhere — and available to downstream users writing their own tests.
entries.rule: Put test doubles that are useful to library consumers in src/, not tests/.

entries.lesson: importlib.reload() is fragile for testing lazy imports
entries.context: A test for ClaudeAdapter used monkeypatch + importlib.reload() to simulate a missing 'anthropic' package. The reload approach has ordering and caching side effects that made the test unreliable.
entries.resolution: Dropped the reload. The lazy import is inside __init__, so monkeypatching sys.modules['anthropic'] = None is sufficient — ClaudeAdapter() will hit the ImportError path on the next instantiation.
entries.rule: When testing lazy imports, monkeypatch sys.modules without reloading the module.

entries.lesson: Optional SDK dependencies need an injected-client testing seam
entries.context: ClaudeAdapter and GeminiAdapter wrap optional SDKs (anthropic, google-generativeai). Testing them without live credentials or complex mocking required a way to inject a pre-built client.
entries.resolution: Added _client / _model_instance constructor parameters. When not None, the adapter skips the SDK import entirely. Tests pass a MagicMock; production code uses the real SDK client.
entries.rule: Any class wrapping an external SDK should accept an optional pre-built client parameter for testing seam injection.

entries.lesson: LLM output format must tolerate separator variants
entries.context: The Observer expected lines like '🔴 text' but LLMs often produce '🔴: text' or '🔴- text'. Tests that hardcoded exact format would miss real LLM outputs.
entries.resolution: The Observer parser strips leading ' :-' characters after the priority emoji with .lstrip(' :-'). Three separator styles are now handled without special-casing.
entries.rule: When parsing LLM free-text output, strip likely separator characters rather than requiring an exact format.

entries.lesson: Reflector must guard against empty LLM responses overwriting the log
entries.context: The Reflector's job is to consolidate and overwrite the observation log. If the LLM returns an empty or unparseable response, a naive implementation would silently destroy all memory.
entries.resolution: reflect() returns False and leaves the log untouched when zero entries are parsed from a non-empty LLM response. Only overwrites when at least one valid entry is recovered.
entries.rule: Any destructive write that depends on LLM output needs a sanity check — refuse to proceed if the output is unparseable.

entries.lesson: Gemini system_instruction is not universally supported
entries.context: google-generativeai's system_instruction parameter is not available on all Gemini model variants. Using it unconditionally causes runtime errors on some models.
entries.resolution: GeminiAdapter prepends the system prompt to the first user message content as a reliable fallback that works across all Gemini model variants.
entries.rule: When targeting multiple model variants of a provider, use the lowest-common-denominator API surface.

entries.lesson: relative field should be computed at build time, not stored
entries.context: The PRD's three-date model includes a 'relative' field (e.g. '3_days_ago'). If stored on disk, it becomes stale immediately and misleads the model on future runs.
entries.resolution: ObservationEntry.serialize() omits 'relative'. ContextBuilder calls entry.render() which computes it fresh from date.today(). The parser's regex accepts and ignores any stored 'relative:' field for forward compatibility.
entries.rule: Never persist derived temporal values; compute them at render time.

entries.lesson: Path.mkdir(mode=0o700) does not guarantee 700 permissions
entries.context: The PRD requires memory/ to be created with 700 (owner-only) permissions. Path.mkdir(mode=0o700) applies the mode before the process umask, so the actual permissions depend on the user's umask setting.
entries.resolution: Call parent.chmod(0o700) explicitly after mkdir(). This overwrites whatever umask applied and guarantees the intended permissions.
entries.rule: Always call chmod() explicitly after mkdir() when a specific permission mode is required.

entries.lesson: Blank-line delimiter works for single-paragraph observations but breaks on multi-paragraph text
entries.context: The observation log uses re.split(r'\n{2,}', ...) to split entries. This is clean and simple but means observation text cannot contain blank lines without being misread as two entries.
entries.resolution: Accepted as a Phase 0 constraint: observation text is single-paragraph. The Observer prompt instructs one sentence per entry. A richer delimiter (e.g. a sentinel line) can be introduced in Phase 3 if needed.
entries.rule: Document format constraints explicitly; defer delimiter complexity until there's a concrete use case that requires it.

entries.lesson: SSH config pointed to a non-existent key file for github.com
entries.context: The project machine's ~/.ssh/config had IdentityFile ~/.ssh/tommy for github.com, but that key did not exist. The first git push attempt failed with 'no such identity'.
entries.resolution: Used GIT_SSH_COMMAND override to push with the existing id_gemini key for the first push; user subsequently fixed the SSH config to point to the correct key.
entries.rule: Verify SSH key paths resolve before relying on them for CI or remote pushes.
